summary: Created a priority-sequenced 8-phase implementation plan from the research findings. High-priority doc-accuracy fixes first: Phase 1 corrects the confirmed nandi/hamsa CPU-vendor factual bug in root README.md; Phase 2 de-stales docs/configuration.md + docs/unstable-packages.md (removes 'planned'/'pending Phase 2' language now that the reorg landed); Phase 3 repoints docs/how-to-add-package.md + docs/how-to-add-service.md at the modules/system + modules/home layout and documents the optional/host-toggled pattern; Phase 4 corrects packages/README.md + docs/packages.md (deletes nonexistent marker-pdf.nix, adds opencode/kooha/slidev, python312->python3, fixes the deleted-file reference). Medium: Phase 5 sweeps emoji from eligible docs (excludes niri.md and the two Ryzen docs). Low/conservative: Phase 6 makes comment-only cleanups in modules/system/{packages,desktop}.nix + home.nix, verified by nix flake check (no functional or stateVersion changes); Phase 7 creates the missing overlays/README.md. Phase 8 is explicitly DEFERRED and not executed autonomously: Ryzen doc consolidation, niri 'testing phase' framing, and the large niri.md emoji strip all require user confirmation. Doc-only vs config changes are kept separate; every phase lists specific files, concrete changes, and a verification step (doc re-read against live config; nix flake check for .nix edits). Phases 1-4, 6, 7 touch disjoint files (parallelizable wave 1); Phase 5 depends on 2,3. Estimated 5.5 hours for executable phases.
next_steps: Run /implement 94 to execute the plan (Phase 8 items are deferred pending user confirmation)
